title: Intriguingly Crisp Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A floral and crisp Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb kebabs with sour cream and pickle, reminiscent of nostalgic flavors. This dish is perfect for those looking for a unique and flavorful meal.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together sour cream, pickle juice, olive oil, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs with a side of sour cream and pickle slices.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
